title: pycnandra decandra
intro: Pycnandra decandra, also known as the Kanawa or Mountain White Yam, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, native to the Pacific island nations of New Caledonia and Vanuatu. The roots and tubers of the Pycnandra decandra plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pycnandra decandra include the potential treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal agent. Pycnandra decandra can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations are often used in traditional remedies, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
